repo_prior_art.cite: pid_network_exposure/v1: finite joint-completion LP sharpness for network-exposure ADEs

published_prior_art.cite: manski_1990_nonparametric_bounds + hoshino_yanagi_2024_noncompliance_unknown_interference; finite projection logic is routine, dual-map network-IV target is not covered

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_network_exposure/v1 is the parent; no non-parent catalogue entry has dual treatment/instrument exposure misspecification with IV principal strata

published_prior_art.cite: hoshino_yanagi_2024_noncompliance_unknown_interference + schroder_oprescu_feuerriegel_kallus_2026_networks_partialid; neither subsumes shared-completion nonsharpness for simultaneous IEM/TEM misspecification

soundness_flags.label: Setup / Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The feasible set matches only conditional outcome moments m(P), but P includes D and S; the program must also reproduce the observed take-up/exposure law P(D,S|Z,I) and enforce D_i=d_i(...) on the design support.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1 / Exhibit 9.1
soundness_flags.one_line: The support-gap arithmetic uses q^{D,G}_{i,s,t,1}=1/2, but Section 6 defines q conditional only on S,T,C, giving q_1=1/3 under the stated three-point design; the displayed 5/8<3/4 values do not follow.

upgrade_review.upgrade_delta_one_line: The proposal stays in the parent partial-ID network cluster, adds monotone IV take-up plus a second misspecified exposure channel, and includes a parent-corner reduction, but the exhibit arithmetic needs repair.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add explicit constraints matching the observed law of D and S given Z and I, not only the outcome mean array.
Recompute Exhibit 9.1 under the Section 6 definition of q; if unchanged definitions are intended, the apparent corrected support gap is 7/12 < 2/3 rather than 5/8 < 3/4.
Name the generic-promotion theorem in the proof sketch, e.g. LP value continuity / parametric polytope stability, and state the perturbation neighborhood explicitly.
Add a location-level anchor showing that Hoshino-Yanagi or Nibbering-Oosterveen leave finite sharp bounds under misspecified dual exposure maps unresolved.

recommended_next_step: Patch the D-law feasibility constraints and recompute Exhibit 9.1 before re-review; the novelty kernel otherwise survives.
